$schema_note: Source roster for /scripts/scrape_seminars.py. Each entry declares the venue, its event-list URL, render mode, known hazards, and per-source extraction rules. Selectors and parser functions get filled in as each source is implemented (TODO markers in extraction.status).

$render_modes.static: Plain HTML, fetch with requests, parse with BeautifulSoup.
$render_modes.drupal: Drupal events page. Default sort often shows past events first; URL parameter override required.
$render_modes.javascript: Client-side rendered. Either Playwright headless browser, or per-event detail-page fetch by slug (cheaper when slugs are discoverable from a sitemap or API endpoint).
$render_modes.google-sites: Google Sites layout. Static-ish but with embedded widgets. Parse with care.
$render_modes.rss: Native RSS feed available, fastest path.
$render_modes.ical: Native iCal feed available, fastest path.
$render_modes.api: Native JSON API endpoint discovered. Cheapest path.

sources.name: Centre for Ethics, University of Toronto
hazards: Google Sites occasionally rebuilds layout. Selectors may break without warning.
Embedded widgets may not render in static fetch; may need per-event link follow.

extraction.venue_default: Centre for Ethics, University of Toronto

sources.notes: Documented as most reliable single source for Toronto philosophy lectures. Ethics@Noon-ish weekly series is the workhorse.

sources.name: Jackman Humanities Institute
hazards: Drupal default sort displays past events first. Must override with sort=date_asc URL parameter or filter in parser.
Some events listed without speaker name; speaker only on detail page.

extraction.venue_default: Jackman Humanities Building, 170 St. George St.

sources.name: Munk School of Global Affairs and Public Policy
hazards: Client-side rendered event list. Sitemap-based slug discovery is cheaper than headless browser.
Many events are panels not lectures; type classification per-event.

sources.name: PhilEvents (Toronto + Ontario filter)
hazards: Stale-rollover: some entries persist past their event date with rolled-forward dates. Filter required to exclude any entry without a 2026+ updated_at field or whose date matches a known previous-year pattern.
Cross-listed events from C4E, JHI, Munk produce duplicates; deduplication needed.

sources.name: Toronto Public Library Appel Salon Series
hazards: TPL programs page is large and covers all branches. Filter to Appel Salon + Toronto Reference Library + intellectual-content keywords.

sources.name: ROM Speaks
hazards: Mixed program: ROM Speaks lectures sit alongside non-seminar events (kids programs, exhibition openings). Filter by category.

sources.name: Hart House, University of Toronto
sources.notes: Covers Hart House Lecture (annual named-honorific), chapel-philosophy series, and Hart House Debates. Multiple types from one source.

sources.name: Revue Cinema
hazards: Director-in-attendance flagged explicitly in event titles. String-match works reliably for this venue.
sources.notes: Most reliable cinema source for director-attendance signals.

sources.name: The Royal Theatre
hazards: Mixed program: cinema, comedy, music. Filter to director-Q&A events only.

sources.name: Paradise Theatre
hazards: Director-attendance rarely flagged in event titles. Verification requires fetching detail page and LLM classification on body copy.
Sneak previews and Toronto Theatrical Premieres often have director-attendance but the page does not say so explicitly.

sources.name: Hot Docs Ted Rogers Cinema
sources.notes: Most director-attendance happens during the April-May Festival window. Year-round programming has fewer Q&A events.

sources.name: TIFF Bell Lightbox
hazards: Large calendar covering festival programming, repertory, curated series. Filter to MDFF Selects plus events with explicit Q&A flag.

sources.name: International Festival of Authors (Harbourfront)
sources.notes: Festival window is October-November; year-round programming is thinner.

sources.name: The Power Plant Contemporary Art Gallery
Art Gallery of Ontario
hazards: Mixed program: exhibitions, kids events, member parties. Filter to artist talks, panel discussions, and curator-led conversations.

sources.name: Toronto Philosophy Cafe / Philosophy in the Park
sources.events_url: TODO - discovery method (Meetup search? FB groups? mailing list?)
hazards: Toronto philosophy cafe groups are scattered across Meetup, Facebook Groups, mailing lists, and Eventbrite. Discovery method needs research before extraction can be built.
Many cafes are member-only; filter to publicly-advertised open sessions.

sources.name: The Agora (Seminar Schools)
sources.notes: Saul-hosted outdoor philosophy gathering at Barry Zukerman Amphitheatre, Earl Bales Park. Self-source; pulls from agora page directly.

sources.name: Curated lectures (hand-maintained)
sources.notes: Hand-curated events from data/manual-events.json. Used for lectures the auto-scraper cannot reliably capture (UofT departments without public RSS, special symposia, the Agora self-source) and for archival lectures the curator wants preserved on the seminars page.

deferred_candidates.$note: Categories the dialectic admitted but no concrete source has been identified yet. Surface when discovery method emerges.
conference-keynote: Open-public sessions at academic conferences hosted at UofT, York, TMU
